title: Strong Qualities, Strong Women
objectives: Identify main points and supporting details from a spoken text about inspiring women and their qualities
Cooperate positively with classmates during group listening tasks when discussing qualities shown in the audio

key_concepts.explanation: Three role models discussed by students in a school radio report: Khadija (may Allah be pleased with her) — Islamic figure, admired by Lara. Mama Anisa — educator, admired by Ahmed. Dr. Sara — scientist, admired by Reem. Each woman showed specific qualities through her actions.

Expressing appreciation for someone's qualities and actions. Each card includes: the woman's name, one action she did, one quality she showed, why her action helped others, one linker (because, so, as a result). Expressions: Thank you for…, We appreciate your…, Your kindness helped…, Your actions made people feel…, You supported others by…

exercises.problem: Look at the pictures and discuss: What important jobs and roles do women have in society? What qualities make a woman a strong and inspiring role model? How can women help and influence their families and society?

Listen to the school radio report. Complete the table: Student → Role Model → Job/Role → Quality Shown → Why the Student Admires Her. Lara → Khadija → Islamic figure → ___ → ___. Ahmed → Mama Anisa → educator → ___ → ___. Reem → Dr. Sara → scientist → ___ → ___.
exercises.answer: Based on listening — qualities and reasons for admiration

exercises.problem: 1. Why do kindness and patience make an educator a strong role model? 2. Why are the women in the radio report strong role models for others? Explain using their actions.

In groups, design three small appreciation cards, one for each woman in the radio programme. Each card must include: the woman's name, one action she did, one quality she showed, why her action helped others, one linker (because, so, as a result). Choose three students to present cards to the class.
Reflect together: Which appreciation card expresses the message most clearly? Why? Which card shows the strongest link between the action and the quality?

teaching_notes: Listening lesson — school radio report about three inspiring women: Khadija (Islamic figure), Mama Anisa (educator), Dr. Sara (scientist). Audio track 7.1. Focus on identifying qualities and their impact. Post-listening: appreciation card design activity. Expressions of Appreciation provided. Linker Bank continues. QR code for audio. 1 page.
